event_name: Multiple @immobiliarelabs Backstage Plugins Compromised on npm
confidence_reason: StepSecurity published primary analysis with the affected package/version table, GitHub issue #1052 records the maintainer-notification thread and affected versions, npm registry metadata confirms simultaneous June 26 patch releases and tarball shasums, and static tarball comparison confirms binding.gyp plus a new 4,991,856-byte index.js in @immobiliarelabs/backstage-plugin-gitlab@2.1.2.

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: npm install invokes node-gyp through binding.gyp; the gyp source shell expansion runs node index.js
payload_behavior: credential harvesting from GitHub Actions secrets, cloud provider keys, and package registry tokens as reported by StepSecurity
attempted persistence in AI coding assistant configuration files as reported by StepSecurity
Bun runtime downloader and obfuscated credential harvesting payload according to StepSecurity's static analysis

provenance.artifact_comparison: @immobiliarelabs/backstage-plugin-gitlab@2.1.2 adds package/binding.gyp and a 4,991,856-byte package/index.js compared with @immobiliarelabs/backstage-plugin-gitlab@2.1.1

open_questions: Public sources reviewed for this post do not yet confirm the exact initial access path used to publish the malicious npm releases.
Removal or maintainer-republished clean versions were not confirmed in the reviewed registry metadata at publication time.

defender_takeaways.detection: Search lockfiles, package caches, CI logs, and Backstage deployment histories for the exact affected @immobiliarelabs package versions and tarball shasums.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Treat node-gyp execution that resolves these package versions as install-time execution, not merely dependency presence.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Rotate GitHub, npm, cloud, registry, Backstage deployment, and developer workstation credentials reachable from environments that installed affected versions.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Block lifecycle/build hooks for dependency install in untrusted contexts and alert on new binding.gyp files in packages that historically had no native build step.

containment_complete: Affected package versions removed from manifests, lockfiles, package caches, internal mirrors, Backstage build images, and deployment pipelines.
Hosts or runners that installed affected versions isolated or rebuilt from clean images.
eradication_complete: No remaining @immobiliarelabs compromised versions, tarball shasums, binding.gyp additions, or node index.js execution records in scoped evidence.
AI coding assistant configuration files on exposed systems checked for unexpected persistence entries.
recovery_complete: Backstage deployments rebuilt with clean package versions or packages removed until maintainer-confirmed fixed releases are available.
GitHub, npm, cloud, registry, and deployment credentials reachable from exposed systems rotated from clean workstations.
closure_required: Downstream GitHub, cloud, npm, and registry audit logs reviewed for unauthorized activity after 2026-06-26T15:00:49Z.
Evidence package retains positive or negative results from repository, lockfile, cache, CI, and runtime telemetry searches.
